taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the forearm and hand.
textField:
Carpal tunnel syndrome is caused by compression of the *median* nerve as it passes deep to the *flexor retinaculum* at the wrist.

Wrist drop is caused by injury to the *radial* nerve, resulting in inability to extend the wrist and fingers.

Claw hand deformity results from injury to the *ulnar* nerve, causing hyperextension at the MCP joints and flexion at the IP joints.

The only superficial flexor NOT supplied by the median nerve is *flexor carpi ulnaris*, which is supplied by the ulnar nerve.

The anatomical *snuffbox* is a depression at the base of the thumb with the *scaphoid* bone on its floor.

The *anterior interosseous* nerve (branch of median) supplies the deep flexors of the lateral fingers and pronator quadratus.

The *palmar* interossei adduct the fingers (PAD), while the *dorsal* interossei abduct them (DAB).

The thenar muscles are supplied by the *recurrent* branch of the median nerve.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review forearm and hand anatomy and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of the forearm and hand!
